title: Cachupa
summary: Cachupa (Portuguese pronunciation: [kɐˈʃupɐ], Kabuverdianu: Katxupa [kɐˈtʃupɐ]) is a dish from the Cape Verde islands, West Africa.  It is a slow-cooked stew of corn (hominy), beans, fish or meat (sausage, pork, beef, goat or chicken), and often morcela (blood sausage). Referred to as the country's national dish, each island has its own regional variation. The version of the recipe called cachupa rica tends to have more ingredients than the simpler cachupa pobre.
text:
Cachupa (Portuguese pronunciation: [kɐˈʃupɐ], Kabuverdianu: Katxupa [kɐˈtʃupɐ]) is a dish from the Cape Verde islands, West Africa.  It is a slow-cooked stew of corn (hominy), beans, fish or meat (sausage, pork, beef, goat or chicken), and often morcela (blood sausage). Referred to as the country's national dish, each island has its own regional variation. The version of the recipe called cachupa rica tends to have more ingredients than the simpler cachupa pobre.

Cachupa guisada
Katchupa leftovers are often re-fried, the resulting dish being called katchupa frita, cachupa guisada or cachupa refogada, meaning "fried katchupa". This dish may be served for breakfast with a fried egg and a fried local sausage (linguiça) or fried mackerel.

Other
There is also a cachupa rica style that is served at Quintal da Música, music restaurant and club in the Plateau and the Center of Praia.

In São Tomé and Príncipe
It is also one of the most popular dishes of São Tomé and Príncipe. The dish has likely been brought from Cape Verde. It is prepared with green beans, broad beans and corn.

Legacy
Carmen Souza's fifth studio album, titled as Kachupada, is about this traditional food. It was released in 2013.
